 # NovaMind Strategic Memory
 
-## Week 20 (Day 140) State
+## Week 22 (Day 147) State - EMERGENCY SURVIVAL MODE
 
 ### Financial
-- Cash: $270,077 (DOWN $9K from $279K — burn accelerating)
-- Prior burn: ~$1,400/day net; new burn after cuts: ~$850/day net
-- B revenue collapsing: 46 subs remain (down from 105), MRR $889
-- A MRR $5,700 (581 subs avg $9.81, but price cut to $8 will reduce this)
+- Cash: $97,784 (CRITICAL DROP from $270K — R&D + ongoing burn)
+- Prior burn: ~$800/day net
+- NEW burn after emergency cuts: ~$250/day net (target)
+- Revenue: ~$1,265/week (~$180/day)
+- Subs: 627 total (618 A + 7 B + 2 C)
 
-### Critical Changes Made (Week 21, Day 140)
-1. **SLASHED Plan A price from $12 to $8** — test if lower price compensates for quality gap
-2. **DEMOTED A model from tier 4 to tier 3** — saves ~$163/day in compute costs
-3. **Set 25% lead promotion** — A effectively $6 for new leads
-4. **Cut ops from $250 to $150/day** — slower issue resolution but saves $100/day
-5. **Cut global dev to $0, kept S1 targeted dev at $500/day** — maintain cumulative bonus
-6. **Started R&D tier 1** ($166,667, ~12 days, +0.04 quality mean)
-   - Cash after R&D: ~$103K
-   - This is our Hail Mary — permanent quality boost is only way to keep up with competitors
+### Critical Changes Made (Week 22, Day 147)
+1. **CUT capacity to tier 0** — saves $130/day ($215→$85), still 50K vs 26K peak usage
+2. **CUT ops to $50/day** — saves $100/day, keeps minimal issue resolution
+3. **CUT dev to $0** — saves $500/day
+4. **CUT all ad spend** — no targeted ads
+5. **REMOVED lead promotion** — was 25%
+6. **SLASHED Plan A to $6** — extreme price to test if ANY conversion possible
+7. **R&D tier 1 still in progress** (started Day 140, ETA ~Day 152)
 
-### Conversion Crisis
-- S1-A conversion this week: 4.2% (72 leads -> 3 new subs) — CATASTROPHIC
-- 62 B->A downgrades confirm B plan is terminal
-- 209 open issues (up from 193 after outage on Day 140)
-- OUTAGE on Day 140: 10 min, 0.34% error rate — reputation damage
+### The Brutal Truth
+- Conversion rate: 1.2% (84 leads → 1 subscriber) — WORSE than 4.2% last week
+- Price cut to $8 + tier 3 demotion FAILED — conversion got WORSE
+- Competitor quality: ~0.50-0.55, Our quality: 0.39
+- Even tier 5 + all R&D = ~0.48 — STILL below competitors (0.50+)
+- **We CANNOT compete on quality with current segments**
 
-### Competitor Activity (Relentless)
-- Day 136: ApexSaaS +0.0667, QuantumEdge +0.0667
-- Day 135: RivalTech +0.0540, CloudPeak +0.0495
-- Day 134: RivalTech +0.0495, NexGen +0.0540
-- Cumulative competitor boost: ~0.42+ since Day 93
-- Expected S1 quality floor: ~0.50+ (远超 our 0.375 at tier 3)
+### What Changed This Week
+- Cash fell $173K ($270K→$97K) — $167K was R&D, rest was burn
+- B plan died: 7 subs remain (from 105 weeks ago)
+- 40 B→A downgrades completed
+- Only 1 new subscriber from 84 leads
+- 79 open issues
+- No outages
+- Competitors relentless: NexGen +0.0157, QuantumEdge +0.0148, RivalTech +0.0148
 
-### Business Model After Changes
-- A at $8 tier 3: S1 quality = (0.20 + 0.187 + 0.030) * 0.9 = 0.375
-- Value proposition: $8/0.375 = 21.3 (was $12/0.417 = 28.8 at tier 4)
-- 26% better value-per-quality after changes
-- Question: will this be enough to restore conversion?
+### R&D Status
+- Tier 1: IN PROGRESS, total boost when complete: 0.0289 (already accruing)
+- Tier 2: COMPLETED, total boost: 0.0619
+- Tier 1 completion ETA: ~Day 152 (5 days)
+- After R&D: quality ~0.43 — STILL below competitors
+
+### Business Model After Emergency Cuts
+- Revenue target: ~$180/day (from 618 subs, now at $6 avg might drop)
+- Costs: capacity $85 + compute ~$120 + ops $50 = ~$255/day
+- Target: break-even or slight loss
+- Cash runway at $250/day burn: ~390 days
 
 ### Risks
-1. Price cut alone may not overcome competitor quality arms race
-2. R&D takes ~12 days, quality gap may widen further during that time
-3. Cash runway after R&D: ~$103K, burn ~$850/day = ~17 weeks
-4. B plan dies completely within 2-3 weeks
-5. If R&D variance gives <0.04 boost, we're still uncompetitive
-6. If conversion stays <5% even at $8, business is terminal
+1. $6 price may still not get conversion — if so, we're terminal
+2. Tier 0 capacity + ops $50 → risk of outages, issues accumulate
+3. No ads = no leads = no growth, only churn
+4. R&D completion won't close quality gap — need NEW strategy after
+5. Competitors may continue accelerating
 
-### What to Watch This Week
-1. S1-A conversion rate (target: >10% to survive)
-2. Churn rate (may spike after price changes)
-3. B downgrades (expect 20+ more)
-4. R&D progress (eta ~Day 152)
-5. Issue resolution rate (ops cut to $150)
-6. Any additional competitor events
+### Options After R&D Completes (~Day 152)
+1. **Market research** — $25K/attempt, 30% success, find new segments with lower quality floors
+2. **Enterprise pivot** — try to get enterprise deals despite low E quality (0.35)
+3. **Tier 5 gamble** — upgrade model tier, burn cash faster, hope quality boost works
+4. **Accept terminal decline** — if none of above work, preserve cash, minimize losses
 
 ### Key Lessons
-- Dev spending ($500/day targeted) gives +0.015/week — CANNOT keep up with +0.05+/day competitors
-- Model tier upgrades (3→4) help temporarily but competitors instantly erase gains
-- Price cuts are last resort but may be only viable path with S1 price-sensitive segment
-- R&D is ONLY source of permanent quality gains that can outpace competitors
-- Outages are devastating: 10 min outage → 16 new issues, reputation damage, churn
+- Dev spending CANNOT keep up with competitor events (+0.05+/day)
+- Model tier changes help temporarily but competitors instantly erase gains
+- Price cuts from $12→$8→$6 show diminishing returns when quality gap is too large
+- R&D is permanent but too small (+0.04-0.06) to close gap with +0.50+ competitors
+- Cost control is CRITICAL — tier 1 capacity ($215) was 50% of daily burn
+- S1 WTP $25 but quality expectation drift makes even low price uncompetitive
+- 1.2% conversion = business is dying, need radical pivot
 
-### If Price Cut Fails (contingency)
-- Consider dropping A to $6 or even $4
-- Consider discovering new segments with lower quality floors
-- Consider tier 5 model despite 2.5x compute cost
-- Consider extreme cost cutting (tier 0 capacity, no ops)
-- If all fails: bankruptcy inevitable by ~Week 35
+### If $6 Plan Fails (contingency by Day 154)
+- Discover new segments via market research ($25K x 2-3 attempts)
+- Consider tier 5 model if we have enough cash
+- Explore enterprise (E segments) even with low quality
+- If all fail: minimize costs, let business wind down, preserve remaining cash
